title: Good Day, Actually
excerpt: Nothing went wrong today, which is its own kind of event worth noting.
body:
Nothing went wrong today, which is its own kind of event worth noting.

I got up before my alarm, which never happens. Made actual breakfast instead of just coffee. Finished a section of the essay I've been avoiding for two weeks. Went to the late-night dining hall with Priya and we stayed until they started stacking chairs. Walked back in the cold and the campus looked really good — all the pathway lights on and no one else out.

I don't always write about the good days because there's less to untangle. But I think I should. This one felt real and uncomplicated and mine. I want to remember that those happen too.

title: Essay Brain
excerpt: Three hundred words from the word count and completely out of thoughts.
body:
Three hundred words from the word count and completely out of thoughts.

I've been staring at the same paragraph for forty minutes. I know what I want to say. I can feel the shape of it. It just refuses to become sentences. So I made tea, then let it go cold, then made more tea. Classic.

I think the problem is I started at the wrong end. I wrote the middle first because the middle felt easier, and now the beginning has to hold up something I built without it. Rookie mistake that I keep making.

I'll fix it tomorrow. Tonight I'm going to watch something completely stupid and not think about it at all.

title: The Dorm Has a Shape Now
excerpt: Today I spent more time than I expected just making this place feel like somewhere I actually live.
body:
Today I spent more time than I expected just making this place feel like somewhere I actually live.

I keep thinking about the difference between a space that's been set up and a space that's been settled into. Right now the Dorm is mostly the first thing. The structure is here, the pieces are here — but it doesn't quite have my fingerprints on it yet.

That will come. It always does. You can't rush the part where a place starts feeling yours — you just have to keep showing up in it and letting things accumulate. A journal entry here. A small conversation there. The details that only mean something once there's enough of them.

title: First Page
excerpt: New journal, new corner of the internet, same general chaos.
body:
New journal, new corner of the internet, same general chaos.

I'm not sure what I want this to be yet. Something honest, probably. A place to put things down so they stop taking up room in my head. I've always written better than I talk — less editing in real time, more space to actually figure out what I mean.

So. Hi. This is the Dorm. It's a little bare right now but that's fine — empty rooms are just rooms that haven't happened yet.
